dataset.name: 10x Multiome GEM-X 10k human brain
dataset.tissue: Human brain; top 15% immune-scoring cells removed before regulon scoring

biological_sanity.method: Top regulons by F-statistic of AUCell scores across leiden clusters; cell-type discrimination signal, after subsetting the top 15% immune-scoring cells
biological_sanity.scope: Top-10 check only; full 1,748-regulon set name-presence against the canonical list requires the regulons.json output which is not in this PR

scope_notes: Stages exercised: GRN, topics, AUCell. Cistarget, enhancer-link, and eRegulon assembly not run (motif_rankings and gene_coords not supplied).

Methodology: top 15% immune-scoring cells removed before scoring to surface neuronal signal. PR description notes this shifts top-discriminating regulons from immune (FLI1) toward neuronal (SRRM3, CELF4).
Before-and-after comparison: artefact records the AFTER-subsetting top-10 only. Before-subsetting top-10 is referenced in the PR body but not in the JSON.
Biology check is name-presence within the top-10 by F-stat only. Full 1,748-regulon set check requires the regulons.json output which is not in this PR.
env.cpu set to null: the original artefact recorded x86_64 (architecture only, not CPU model), and env.cpu in the schema denotes CPU model. WSL2 environment is captured in env.os.
Input file hashes (rna_h5ad_md5, atac_fragments_md5_first_8mb, peaks_bed_md5), n_cpus, and ram_gb are null; can be filled by Liudmyla on her end.
